reasoning: Paul Biedermann's 200m long course freestyle record was set in 2009 during the super-suit era and is considered extremely secure. No active swimmer is close (the closest recent performance is David Popovici's 1:42.97 in relatively isolated circumstances, while Biedermann's record is 1:42.00). Furthermore, there are almost no major long course competitions between October and January, as the winter season focuses primarily on 25m short course events. Thus, the chance of the record being broken on the field of play in the next 3 months is practically zero. The extremely small residual probability (which increases slightly over time) allows for freak administrative changes (e.g., World Aquatics retroactively altering super-suit rules) or significant Wikipedia vandalism persisting long enough to be recorded on the resolution date.
batch_queries: According to Wikipedia, will Paul Biedermann still hold the world record for 200m freestyle in long course (50 metres) swimming pools on each resolution date listed below?
